content: The spelling variant 'reflexion' (a dated alternative of reflection that carries linguistic memory of older orthographic conventions) can act as a natural 'mirror' trigger for LLM self-reflection: prompting an LLM with 'reflexion' (instead of the modern 'reflection') activates dormant multi-era linguistic context, pushing the model to compare its initial response against the implicit expectations of older and modern discourse norms to uncover inconsistent reasoning. This small orthographic shift creates the inconsistent solving perspective that cutting-edge LLM self-reflection mechanisms like Self-Contrast rely on to improve output quality.
